struct Point
  x, y, z : Int64

    other.is_a?(Point) &&

  end

def solve(points : Array(Array(Int64)), target : Array(Int64)) : Int64
  
  target_p = Point.new(target[0], target[1], target[2])
  
  # 1. Initialization: Build the set of all known points (S0)
  all_points = Set(Array(Int64)).new
  
  initial_points_as_points = points.map { |p| Point.new(p[0], p[1], p[2]) }
  
  initial_point_set = Set(Point).new
  initial_point_set.each do |p|
    initial_point_set.add(p)
  end
  
  # Check generation 0
  if initial_point_set.include?(target_p)
    return 0
  end
  
  all_points.add_all(initial_point_set.to_a)
  
  k = 1
  
  loop do
    newly_generated_points = Set(Point).new
    
    # Get current set of all unique points (S_{k-1})
    # We iterate over the list representation to easily get indices for distinct pairing
    current_points_list = all_points.to_a
    n = current_points_list.size
    
    # Generate candidates for generation k
    # We iterate over all ordered pairs (a, b) where a != b (by coordinate)
    # Since all_points contains unique coordinates, iterating over distinct indices ensures distinct coordinates.
    
    new_candidates_found = false
    
    (0...n).each do |i|
      (0...n).each do |j|
        if i == j
          next # Points must be different
        end
        
        p1 = current_points_list[i]
        p2 = current_points_list[j]
        
        # Calculate c = [floor((x1 + x2) / 2), ...]
        c_x = (p1.x + p2.x) / 2
        c_y = (p1.y + p2.y) / 2
        c_z = (p1.z + p2.z) / 2
        
        c = Point.new(c_x, c_y, c_z)
        
        # Only consider new unique points
        if !all_points.include?(c)
          newly_generated_points.add(c)
          new_candidates_found = true
        end
      end
    end
    
    # Check if we generated any new points
    if !newly_generated_points.size > 0 && !new_candidates_found
      # If we found no new points, the process stagnated, and target was not found
      break
    end
    
    # Update the set of all points (S_k = S_{k-1} U N_k)
    all_points.add_all(newly_generated_points.to_a)
    
    # Check if target was generated in this step k
    if newly_generated_points.include?(target_p)
      return k
    end
    
    k += 1
    
    # Safety break, although theoretically unnecessary given finite state space (7^3=343 states)
    if k > 100 { 
      # This limit is arbitrary but serves as a guard against infinite loops in pathological cases, 
      # though given constraints, it should terminate much faster.
      break 
    }
  end
  
  # If the loop finishes without finding the target
  return -1
end
